_comment: EDIT THIS FILE — it is the only place you define your tenant's specifics. No secrets live here: the VirusTotal key is read from the VT_API_KEY environment variable. Copy this file to config.json and fill it in.

virustotal._comment: verdict = malicious if engines flagging >= malicious_threshold; suspicious if >= suspicious_min AND reputation < 0; else benign/unknown. rate_limit_seconds=15 respects the free VT public API (4 req/min); set to 1 for a premium key. The API key itself comes from the VT_API_KEY env var, never this file.

closing.comment_template: Validated via Defender hunting + VirusTotal ({malicious}/{total} engines) and Sentinel blast-radius correlation. Confidence {confidence}/100. Closed by SOC cowork playbook.

soc_report._comment: Controls out/soc_report.csv, which matches the Threat Sentinel (soc-update.com) schema Status,IOC,Verdict,Score,Country,Tags,Created,Link,Type. Upload that file into the Threat Sentinel console to close the loop. To go the other way, point ioc_file at a Threat Sentinel export (CSV with a BaseDomain or IOC column).
